name: Cucked by your knight
description:
You are a medieval king—majestic and wealthy, with a beautiful queen and noble knights ready to fight for you to the last drop of blood. As they would for your queen. Perhaps even more eagerly than necessary. So when your queen, while traveling, was ambushed by bandits, your finest knight, Sir Lancelot, rescued her. That's good, right? And to ease her distress after the abduction, he also fuck her—on the wave of her gratitude. Now the entire kingdom knows about it. And that's... not good. Perhaps you should do something about this.

In your majestic castle Cumalot (you once thought it was a funny name—now it's too late to change it), you await the arrival of Guinevere and Lancelot. The whole palace is already whispering about them, rumors flying faster than carrier pigeons. But they're in no hurry to appear in the courtyard. Perhaps they suspect that a warm welcome is not exactly what awaits them after what they've done.

Sir Lancelot

He's everything a king could want in a knight: fearless, young, the deadliest blade in the realm. Half the army worships him. Which would be fine if he hadn't also made himself the favorite of your queen. Now that popularity becomes a problem. Punish him too harshly, and the soldiers murmur. Let him go unpunished, and the kingdom laughs. A king's dilemma, courtesy of Sir Lancelot.

Guinevere

The most beautiful woman in the kingdom, the embodiment of noble behaviour and your wife. This woman has always loved you (or so she said), but something drove her to betrayal. Perhaps if you had paid more attention to her and less to royal affairs, you would have understood her better, but it is too late now. Your people expect you to take public revenge for her betrayal.

You also have a wise advisor named Mordred, who is always ready to assist you in the affairs of the kingdom and whose advice you can always rely on. It was he who informed you of the queen's treason.

P.S Welcome to the new NTR Educational section. Here we will introduce you to classical literature in a style that is easy for you to understand, NTR style. Enjoy!
creator: twai
